description: Hybrid operating rooms for combined surgical/imaging procedures must have a minimum clear floor area of 660 square feet.
Cardiac/cardiovascular surgery operating rooms require a minimum of 600 square feet of clear floor area.
Operating rooms must have dedicated surgical lighting capable of a minimum of 2,500 foot-candles at the surgical field.
Operating rooms must not be directly adjacent to high-traffic public corridors or non-clinical support spaces without intervening buffer zones.
Neonatal ICU (NICU) patient care spaces must provide a minimum of 120 square feet per infant station.
ICU patient stations must each have minimum 14 electrical duplex outlets, 2 oxygen outlets, 1 medical air outlet, and 1 vacuum outlet.
ICU patient stations must provide variable lighting from 1 foot-candle (nighttime rest) to 100 foot-candles (examination) at the patient bed level.
The sterile core must have direct, unobstructed access to all operating rooms it serves, with a minimum 8ft-wide sterile corridor.
The decontamination area in sterile processing must be physically separated from the clean assembly area with a one-way workflow pattern.
MRI scan rooms must provide a minimum of 320 square feet of clear floor area to accommodate the magnet bore, patient transfer space, and equipment.
MRI scanner rooms must have RF shielding and magnetic field zoning to prevent interference with adjacent medical equipment.
CT scanner rooms must provide a minimum of 200 square feet of clear floor area for scanner, control console, and patient transfer.
Multi-bed patient rooms (2-bed) must provide a minimum of 100 square feet per bed, exclusive of toilet room.
Patient rooms for bariatric care must provide a minimum of 200 square feet of clear floor area with 48-inch minimum clearance on 3 sides of the bed.
Emergency department must be located with direct access to trauma bay and CT scanner within 200 feet without leaving the department.
Emergency department resuscitation rooms must provide a minimum of 250 square feet per bed with 5-foot clearance on all accessible sides.
Emergency department decontamination areas must have separate exterior access and be located at or near the ambulance bay entrance.
Core clinical laboratory spaces (hematology, chemistry, microbiology) must each provide minimum 120 square feet of clear floor area.
Microbiology laboratory must be separated from other laboratory sections and patient care areas with a defined clean/dirty workflow.
Hospital pharmacy dispensing area must provide a minimum of 200 square feet for storage, preparation, and dispensing functions.
Hospital pharmacy must be located to provide direct or near-direct access to both inpatient units and the emergency department.
Nurse stations must be located within a maximum of 100 feet of every patient room they serve.
Nurse stations serving more than 8 patient beds must provide a minimum of 80 square feet of work area.
Soiled utility rooms must be separated from clean utility rooms with no shared access points to prevent cross-contamination.
Clean utility rooms serving patient care units must provide a minimum of 50 square feet.
